---
title: "Turtle analysis"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
#install.packages(c("readxl", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "plotly", "leaflet","leaflet.extras")
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(leaflet)
library(leaflet.extras)
# Read the data
data <- read_excel("C:\\Users\\tawheed\\Downloads\\turtle.xlsx", sheet = "data")
names(data)
data$`Body condition`[is.na(data$`Body condition`)] <- "Unknown"
data$`Decomposition Level`[is.na(data$`Decomposition Level`)] <- "Not Applicable"
# Filter data from 2018
data_2018 <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 2018)
dead_data <- data_2018 %>%
  filter(`Mortality Status` == "Dead")
species_year <- data %>%
  count(year, `Type of Turtle`)
# Distribution of Mortality Status
mortality_data <- data_2018 %>%
  count(`Mortality Status`)
plot_ly(mortality_data,
        labels = ~`Mortality Status`,
        values = ~n,
        type = "pie",
        textinfo = "label+percent")
# Distribution by Type of Turtles
species_plot <- ggplot(data, 
                       aes(x = `Type of Turtle`, 
                           fill = `Type of Turtle`)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Turtle Species",
       x = "Type of Turtle",
       y = "Number of Turtles",
       fill = "Species") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Green turtle" = "green",
    "Hawksbill Turtle" = "orange",
    "Loggerhead turtle" = "blue",
    "Leatherback turtle" = "brown",
    "Olive Ridley" = "purple",
    "Unknown" = "gray",
    "Al rimani turtle" = "black"
  )) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(species_plot)
# Spatial Distribution by Wilayat
wilayat_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = Wilayat)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Turtles by Wilayat",x = "Wilayat",y = "Number of Turtle") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(wilayat_plot)
# Distribution by Year
data$year <- as.numeric(data$year)
year_summary <- data %>%
  count(year)
year_plot <- ggplot(year_summary, aes(x = year, y = n)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = year_summary$year) +
  labs(title = "Number of Turtle Observations by Year",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Number of Turtle") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(year_plot)


#Distribution by Quarter
quarter_plot <- ggplot(data, 
                       aes(x = quarter, fill = quarter)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Turtle Observations by Quarter",x = "Quarter",y = "Number of Turtles",fill = "Quarter") +
  theme_minimal()
ggplotly(quarter_plot)
# Distribution of Decomposition Level
dead_data$`Decomposition Level` <- factor(
  dead_data$`Decomposition Level`,
  levels = c("Fresh", "Swollen", "Decomposed", "Skeleton"))
decomposition_plot <- ggplot(dead_data, 
                             aes(x = `Decomposition Level`, fill = `Decomposition Level`)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Decomposition Level of Dead Turtles (2018–2024)",
       x = "Decomposition Level",
       y = "Number of Dead Turtles",
       fill = "Decomposition Level") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Fresh" = "green",
  "Swollen" = "orange",
  "Decomposed" = "red",
  "Skeleton" = "black"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(decomposition_plot)
# Distribution of Body Condition
body_data <- dead_data %>%
  count(`Body condition`)
plot_ly(body_data,
        labels = ~`Body condition`,
        values = ~n,
        type = "pie",
        textinfo = "label+percent") %>%
  layout(title = "Body Condition of Dead Turtles (2018–2024)")
# decomposition level by turtle species
species_decomposition_plot <- ggplot(dead_data, aes(x = `Type of Turtle`, fill = `Decomposition Level`)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Decomposition Level by Turtle Species",
       x = "Type of Turtle",
       y = "Number of Turtles") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(species_decomposition_plot)
# Trend of turtle by species over time
species_year <- species_year %>%
  mutate(`Type of Turtle` = tolower(trimws(`Type of Turtle`))) %>%
  mutate(`Type of Turtle` = recode(`Type of Turtle`,
    "hawksbill turtle" = "hawksbill",
    "green turtle" = "green",
    "loggerhead turtle" = "loggerhead",
    "leatherback turtle" = "leatherback",
    "olive ridley" = "olive ridley",
    "other" = "al rimani",
    "unknown" = "unknown"))
species_year$year_factor <- factor(species_year$year)
species_trend_plot <- ggplot(species_year, 
                            aes(x = year_factor, y = n, 
                                group = `Type of Turtle`, 
                                color = `Type of Turtle`)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Trend of Turtle Observations by Species Over Time",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Number of Turtles",
       color = "Species") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "green" = "green",
    "hawksbill" = "orange",
    "loggerhead" = "blue",
    "leatherback" = "brown",
    "olive ridley" = "purple",
    "unknown" = "gray",
    "al rimani" = "black"
  )) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggplotly(species_trend_plot)
# Oman map
location_freq <- data %>%
  group_by(Wilayat, latitude, longitude) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop")
pal <- colorNumeric(palette = "YlOrRd", domain = location_freq$count)
leaflet(location_freq) %>%
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>%  
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = ~sqrt(count) * 3,   
    stroke = FALSE,
    fillColor = ~pal(count),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~paste0("Wilayat: ", Wilayat),
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>Wilayat:</b> ", Wilayat,
      "<br><b>Turtle Count:</b> ", count,
      "<br><b>Latitude:</b> ", round(latitude, 3),
      "<br><b>Longitude:</b> ", round(longitude, 3)
    )
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = pal,
    values = ~count,
    title = "Number of Turtles",
    opacity = 1
  )
# corresponding analysis
#install.packages("FactoMineR")
#install.packages("factoextra")
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
table_species_decomp <- table(
  dead_data$`Type of Turtle`,
  dead_data$`Decomposition Level`
)
ca_result <- CA(table_species_decomp, graph = FALSE)
fviz_ca_biplot(
  ca_result,
  repel = TRUE
)
# make sure were factors
data_2018 <- data_2018 %>%
  mutate(
    `Mortality Status` = as.factor(`Mortality Status`),
    `Type of Turtle` = as.factor(`Type of Turtle`),
    Wilayat = as.factor(Wilayat),
    `Body condition` = as.factor(`Body condition`)
  )
# Mortality Status × Wilayat
table_wilayat_mortality <- table(data_2018$Wilayat,                                 data_2018$`Mortality Status`)
chisq_wilayat_mortality <- chisq.test (table_wilayat_mortality)
table_wilayat_mortality
# Mortality Status × Type of Turtle
table_species_mortality <- table(data_2018$`Type of Turtle`,
                                 data_2018$`Mortality Status`)
chisq_species_mortality <- chisq.test(table_species_mortality)
table_species_mortality
# Decomposition Level × Type of Turtle
table_species_decomp <- table(dead_data$`Type of Turtle`,                        dead_data$`Decomposition Level`)
chisq_species_decomp <- chisq.test(table_species_decomp)
table_species_decomp
# Body Condition × Mortality Status
table_body_mortality <- table(data$`Body condition`,
                              data$`Mortality Status`)
chisq_body_mortality <- chisq.test(table_body_mortality)
table_body_mortality
# Fisher exact test
fisher_species_mortality <- fisher.test(table_species_mortality)
fisher_species_mortality
fisher_wilayat_mortality <- fisher.test(table_wilayat_mortality)
fisher_wilayat_mortality
fisher_body_mortality <- fisher.test(table_body_mortality)
fisher_body_mortality
fisher_species_decomp <- fisher.test(
  table_species_decomp,
  simulate.p.value = TRUE,
  B = 10000
)
fisher_species_decomp
# Fit Logistic Regression models
data_2018$Mortality_binary <- ifelse(
  data_2018$`Mortality Status` == "Dead", 1, 0
)
# Fit Logistic Regression - model 
glm_model <- glm(
  Mortality_binary ~ year + `Type of Turtle`,
  data = data_2018,
  family = binomial
)
summary(glm_model)

# Fit multinomial model 
library(nnet)
dead_data$`Decomposition Level` <- as.factor(dead_data$`Decomposition Level`)
dead_data$`Type of Turtle` <- as.factor(dead_data$`Type of Turtle`)
dead_data$year <- as.factor(dead_data$year)
dead_data$`Decomposition Level` <- relevel(dead_data$`Decomposition Level`, ref = "Fresh")
multi_model <- multinom(`Decomposition Level` ~ `Type of Turtle` + year,
                        data = dead_data)
summary(multi_model)

```
